hidden_constraints.oracle_response: French rules add -s for most nouns (amis), -x after c/z (seinxeux), and -z for oz/seciez (no examples provided in context, but standard rule applies). The prompt mentions removing 's' internally in irregulars but standard inflection follows suffix tables.

The output section must follow the same case normalization pattern used in the 'case_conversion' module (e.g., C001, C002), specifically ensuring no lowercase conversion on the first letter if it was uppercase, or specific title casing as defined elsewhere.

When input.style is 'table' and input.text contains numbers (e.g., '123'), the output must include those digits exactly as they appear in the string, without replacing them with words or removing them.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the naming convention used in the pascal_case_utils module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Arguments that are strictly alphanumeric with underscores must be split, first letter capitalized, then concatenated without separators, e.g., 'Foo_bar' -> 'FooBar'.

context_pointers.vague_reference: refer to the vowel_harmony_map for portuguese exceptions
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Words ending in 'o' (e.g., 'livro' -> 'livros') or consonants ('radio' -> 'radios') generally add 's', but specific vowels like 'ão' become 'ões'; these mappings are hardcoded in internal config files.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_delimited_table_names.json.description: Convert identifier text to a lowercase underscore-separated storage name without changing numeric characters.

feature1_2_pascal_identifiers.json.description: Convert separated or mixed identifier text to an upper-initial concatenated identifier.

feature1_3_camel_identifiers.json.description: Convert separated or mixed identifier text to a lower-initial concatenated identifier.

feature1_4_title_casing.json.description: Uppercase the first letter of words after configured delimiters while preserving other characters.

feature2_1_english_word_number.json.description: Inflect English words between singular and plural forms, including regular nouns, irregular nouns, compounds, and unchanging words.

feature2_2_french_word_number.json.description: Inflect French words between singular and plural forms, including common suffix rules, invariant forms, and listed irregular forms.

feature2_3_spanish_word_number.json.description: Inflect Spanish words between singular and plural forms, including accented endings, consonant changes, invariant words, and articles.

feature2_4_portuguese_word_number.json.description: Inflect Portuguese words between singular and plural forms, including vowel endings, consonant endings, accented forms, and irregular -ão outcomes.

feature2_5_norwegian_bokmal_word_number..description: Inflect Norwegian Bokmål words between singular and plural forms, including regular endings, alternate endings, and invariant nouns.

feature2_6_turkish_word_number.json.description: Inflect Turkish words between singular and plural forms, including vowel-harmony plural suffixes and personal pronouns.

_constraints.input_condition: input.text is an empty string OR input.style is 'table' OR input.style is 'pascal' OR input.style is 'camel'
_constraints.output_rule: return 'output=' followed by a newline character

_constraints.input_condition: input.style is 'table' and input.text contains letters (e.g., 'FooBar')
_constraints.output_rule: Convert to lowercase, replace camelCase boundaries with underscores, preserve underscores, preserve numeric characters

_constraints.input_condition: input.style is 'pascal' and input.text contains separators (e.g., 'foo_bar')
_constraints.output_rule: Remove separators, capitalize first letter, ensure only the first letter is uppercase

_constraints.input_condition: input.style is 'camel' and input.text contains separators (e.g., 'foo_bar')
_constraints.output_rule: Remove separators, capitalize first letter of subsequent words, resulting first letter must be lowercase

_constraints.input_condition: input.style is 'title' and input.delimiters is omitted (或缺失)
_constraints.output_rule: Capitalization handled by hyphen (-) and space ( ) default boundaries

_constraints.input_condition: input.style is 'title' and input.delimiters is provided
_constraints.output_rule: Capitalize first letter of text and first letter after every character in input.delimiters

_constraints.input_condition: input.style is 'table' and input.text contains numbers
_constraints.output_rule: Numeric characters included in the result, not treated as standalone or removed

_constraints.input_condition: input.language is any supported language and input.direction implies a process that finds no match
_constraints.output_rule: Return the original input wording unchanged

_constraints.input_condition: input.word is an empty string
_constraints.output_rule: Return the result for 'empty input text' (i.e., 'output=' followed by newline)

_constraints.input_condition: input.language is 'english' and input.word ends in pluralizable endings (e.g., 'ability' -> 'abilities')
_constraints.output_rule: Apply plural suffix rule unless it matches an irregular exception

_constraints.input_condition: input.language is 'english', input.word is 'acceptancecriterion' (plural direction)
_constraints.output_rule: Convert 'criterion' to 'criteria' (irregular suffix rule)

_constraints.input_condition: input.language is 'english', input.word is 'acceptancecriteria' (singular direction)
_constraints.output_rule: Remove 's' to form singular (ignoring the 'i' drop inside the word for this test case)

_constraints.input_condition: input.language is 'english', input.word ends in 's' or 'z'
_constraints.--: Show no change in singular form (invariant ending)

_constraints.input_condition: input.language is 'english', input.word is 'abilities' (singular direction)
_constraints.output_rule: Remove the final 's' to reverse the plural

_constraints.input_condition: input.language is 'french'
_constraints.output_rule: Apply French pluralization rules (-s for most, -x for x, -z for z)

_constraints.input_condition: input.language is 'spanish'
_constraints.output_rule: Add -s, -es, or -as depending on vowel ending (o -> o, u -> u, etc.)

_constraints.input_condition: input.language is 'spanish' and input.word is 'señora' (plural direction)
_constraints.output_rule: Change word-final 'a' to 'as' (acativo effect)

_constraints.input_condition: input.language is 'portuguese'
_constraints.output_rule: If word ends in 'o', add 's. If word ends in consonant other than 'o', add 's', but check for vowel harmony changes (e.g. -ão -> -ões)

_constraints.input_condition: input.language is 'portuguese' and input.word is 'radio' (singular vs plural)
_constraints.output_rule: Radio is treated as an excepted word with ending 'o' -> 'os'

_constraints.input_condition: input.language is 'norwegian_bokmal'
_constraints.output_rule: Apply Bokmål pluralization rules (regular sets, -er, -er, etc.)

_constraints.input_condition: input.language is 'turkish'
_constraints.output_rule: Apply vowel harmony to add plural suffix (-lar/-ler), rotate syllable boundary to handle lengthening if necessary

_constraints.input_condition: input.direction is 'plural'

_constraints: result must be consistent with the expected output
